2012-12-01.title: Which Turkey is Healthiest?
2012-12-01.abstract: Is it dangerous for Turkey to bring religion back, or is it necessary as it seeks to become a leader of the Muslim world? Will making the country’s leadership more reflective of the public risk the freedoms for women, artists and gays?

2012-12-01.title: State Secret Revealed: Mitterrand as a Doting Father
2012-12-01.abstract: Mazarine Pingeot, the daughter of François Mitterrand and Anne Pingeot, his longtime mistress, has written a diary that includes her memories of Mr. Mitterrand as an attentive and doting father.

2012-12-01.title: Battle Between Argentine Media Empire and President Heats Up Over a Law
2012-12-01.abstract: A law championed by President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner will force Clarín to divest most of its lucrative cable operations starting in December.

2012-12-01.title: New U.N. Status for Palestinians Could Open Door for Claims of Israeli War Crimes
2012-12-01.abstract: Working with the International Criminal Court could also make Palestinians vulnerable to Israeli counterclaims, experts say.

2012-12-01.title: In South Africa, Lethal Battles for Even Smallest of Political Posts
2012-12-01.abstract: Dozens of officials, including ward councilors, party leaders and mayors, have been killed in what has become a desperate, deadly struggle for power and its spoils.

2012-12-02.title: The Longest  Season Really Is Over
2012-12-02.abstract: 2012 was a marathon for those of us following the race. It took its toll. And just when it seemed it was over, a mini-controversy arose.

2012-12-02.title: Another ‘Nail House’ in China Gets Hammered
2012-12-02.abstract: After long resistance by a Chinese duck farmer and his wife, their “nail house” is demolished.

2012-12-02.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-12-02.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2012-12-02.title: Attackers at U.S.-Afghan Base Wore Coalition Uniforms
2012-12-02.abstract: Disguised in coalition military uniforms, the Taliban fighters  started a two-hour gun battle that killed nine insurgents, four Afghan guards and at least four civilians.

2012-12-02.title: Egyptian Court Postpones Ruling on Constitutional Assembly
2012-12-02.abstract: Judges delayed their much-awaited decision and accused a crowd of Islamists of intimidating them, but what actually took place at the courthouse is a matter of dispute.

2012-12-03.title: Displaced Residents Grapple With Hurdles of Going Home
2012-12-03.abstract: The government has made bringing people back to rural towns a priority, but logistics and fears of violence have complicated matters.

2012-12-03.title: France’s Floating Generation
2012-12-03.abstract: As youth unemployment hits 22 percent, many young people cannot find jobs that get them on the path to being taxpaying, property-owning adults.

2012-12-03.title: Young, Educated and Jobless in France
2012-12-03.abstract: Despite solid credentials, college graduates cannot find permanent jobs that get them on the path to the taxpaying, property-owning French ideal that was once the norm.

2012-12-03.title: Amid Euphoria Over U.N. Vote, Palestinians Still Face Familiar Challenges
2012-12-03.abstract: With Israeli elections scheduled for January, the situation is likely to get tougher for the Palestinians before it gets better.

2012-12-03.title: Collaboration in Gaza Leads to Grisly Fate
2012-12-03.abstract: Recent vigilante-style killings of seven Palestinians suspected of providing intelligence to Israel highlighted the pathetic plight of collaborators.

2012-12-04.title: For Iraq, Year Ends the Way It Began, With Guns Drawn
2012-12-04.abstract: Almost a year after the United States military left Iraq, Baghdad is in crisis mode again, with its army in a standoff with Kurdish security forces in a disputed area near Kirkuk.

2012-12-04.title: Pakistan Reels With Violence Against Shiites
2012-12-04.abstract: More than 100 Hazaras have been killed by Sunni extremist gunmen this year, many in broad daylight.

2012-12-04.title: Brothel Owner Rescues Greek Soccer Team
2012-12-04.abstract: During tough economic times, an amateur team found sponsorship from a local madam.

2012-12-04.title: Assad Suffering Reversals in Fighting and Diplomacy
2012-12-04.abstract: As the battle for the Syrian capital worsened, a Turkish official said that Russia had agreed to try a new approach to persuade President Bashar al-Assad to relinquish power.

2012-12-04.title: In Land of Bailouts, Greek Madam Rescues Local Soccer Team
2012-12-04.abstract: Soula Alevridou’s gift of about $1,300, in part to buy pink training outfits, has caused an uproar as officials debate the appropriateness of having a brothel owner step in.

2012-12-05.title: Trying to Close Orphanages Where Many Aren’t Orphans at All
2012-12-05.abstract: The Haitian government has started a campaign to try to keep parents from sending their children away simply because they cannot afford to support them.

2012-12-05.title: Indian Official Starts Pulling Up Corruption’s Roots in Mumbai
2012-12-05.abstract: Chief Minister Prithviraj Chavan has forced developers in India’s financial capital to pay fees they have long avoided, offering hope to residents.

2012-12-05.title: China: Tibetan Monk Kills Himself in Fiery Protest
2012-12-05.abstract: A monk in a Tibetan region of western China killed himself by setting himself on fire Monday evening to protest Chinese rule, an advocacy group said.

2012-12-05.title: How Crash Cover-Up Altered China’s Succession
2012-12-05.abstract: The effort to suppress news of a deadly Ferrari crash, insiders  now reveal, hastened the decline of the departing president, Hu Jintao, and spurred the ascent of China’s new leader, Xi Jinping.

2012-12-05.title: Queen Noor Expresses Hope on the Rights of Arab Women
2012-12-05.abstract: Amid worries that the revolutions in the Middle East may set back women’s rights, Jordan’s queen urged that it is too soon to give up.

2012-12-06.title: India’s Lower House Votes to Support Foreign Investment
2012-12-06.abstract: The move to back the government’s decision to allow Walmart and other major retailers to set up shop passed after a  nationally televised fight.

2012-12-06.title: Oscar Niemeyer, Architect Who Gave Brasília Its Flair, Dies at 104
2012-12-06.abstract: Mr. Niemeyer, the Brazilian architect, created flowing designs that infused Modernism with a new sensuality and inspired generations of architects worldwide.

2012-12-06.title: An Abduction Shines Light on Ukraine Asylum Policy and on Kremlin Methods
2012-12-06.abstract: Masked men shoved Russian opposition leader Leonid Razvozzhayev into a van, outside the office of his lawyer in Kiev.

2012-12-06.title: British Newspapers Agree to Regulator
2012-12-06.abstract: While a group of editors embraced some recommendations from a recent report, they rejected a state-sanctioned oversight system.

2012-12-06.title: Peace Process Pulls at Germany-Israel Ties
2012-12-06.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel met with  Chancellor Angela Merkel in Berlin, amid evidence of a growing rift between the longtime allies.

2012-12-07.title: South Sudan: Journalist Killed
2012-12-07.abstract: The journalist, Diing Chan Awuol,  had published articles critical of the government, its policy toward Sudan and its ties to Sudanese rebel groups.

2012-12-07.title: For International Students, College Checklists for December
2012-12-07.abstract: College counselors in the United States offer monthly advice for juniors and seniors who want to stay on track during the admissions process.

2012-12-07.title: Futuristic Vision for a City Rooted in the Past
2012-12-07.abstract: The fashion designer Pierre Cardin has a dream of turning a Venice industrial dump into a futuristic palazzo, leading to a debate about the city’s future.

2012-12-07.title: New Commander for Tense Region
2012-12-07.abstract: President Obama intends to nominate Gen. Lloyd J. Austin III to be the top officer of the United States Central Command,  the Pentagon announced Thursday.

2012-12-07.title: Star Publicist in Britain Is Questioned in Sex Case
2012-12-07.abstract: Max Clifford, known for advising pop stars, was the fifth person to be arrested in an inquiry set up in the wake of allegations of widespread sexual abuse by Jimmy Savile.

2012-12-08.title: Prank Call Seeking Royal Family Secrets Takes Horrifying Turn
2012-12-08.abstract: A nurse tricked by Australian D.J.’s who called posing as Queen Elizabeth and Prince Charles to get information about Kate Middleton’s pregnancy was found dead on Friday.

2012-12-08.title: The Best Gift Ideas for the Best-Dressed Global Traveler
2012-12-08.abstract: From office style that goes beyond office drab to the most practical yet stylish travel gear, the I.H.T.’s fashion special report tells you what you need to wear — or give.

2012-12-08.title: Rebel Groups in Syria Make Framework for Military
2012-12-08.abstract: After three days of intensive talks, military commanders of the main Free Syrian Army units from all over Syria agreed Friday to a unified command structure.

2012-12-08.title: Japanese Quake Stirs On-Air Alarm, but No Damage to Nuclear Plants Is Reported
2012-12-08.abstract: After a 7.3-magnitude earthquake hit near the same area affected by last year’s earthquake and tsunami, a television broadcaster urged people to “flee now to save your life!”

2012-12-08.title: An Enemy of Brussels, and Not Afraid to Say So
2012-12-08.abstract: A political party, and its irascible leader, Nigel Farage, has gained on the strength of one idea: Britain needs to leave the European Union.

2012-12-09.title: Syrian Rebels Tied to Al Qaeda Play Key Role in War
2012-12-09.abstract: The United States faces a challenge, as some of the best fighters in an uprising that it wants to support belong to a group that it considers a terrorist organization.

2012-12-09.title: North Korea Cites Rocket Problems
2012-12-09.abstract: Referencing technical issues, North Korea said that it might have to postpone a rocket launching planned for as early as Monday.

2012-12-09.title: Adoption Limbo
2012-12-09.abstract: American families are struggling with long adoption delays in Guatemala.

2012-12-09.title: Chávez Says His Cancer Has Returned and Names His Successor
2012-12-09.abstract: Doctors found malignant cells during tests in Cuba, President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela said, adding that his vice president would succeed him if his health worsened.

2012-12-09.title: Feeding the People in Cuba
2012-12-09.abstract: Last year, President Raúl Castro legalized small agricultural businesses as part of an effort to revive the economy.

2012-12-10.title: For Iran, Unrest in Syria Is Noise, Not Brutal War
2012-12-10.abstract: Iran’s leaders insist that the conflict in Syria, their main ally in the Arabic world, is manageable and ultimately will be resolved to their advantage.

2012-12-10.title: Before More Cancer Surgery, Chávez Had Some Political Fences to Mend at Home
2012-12-10.abstract: President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela postponed another procedure to solidify party support for his chosen successor, Vice President Nicolás Maduro.

2012-12-10.title: Birger Stromsheim, Hero on Skis in an Anti-Nazi Raid, Dies at 101
2012-12-10.abstract: Mr. Stromsheim helped to destroy equipment that the Germans were using to produce what is known as heavy water, a crucial ingredient in making a nuclear weapon.

2012-12-10.title: Signals of a More Open Economy in China
2012-12-10.abstract: Xi Jinping, the new head of the Communist Party, appears to be tapping more deeply into the idea of a national revival than his recent predecessors did.

2012-12-10.title: Mexican-American Singing Star Is Feared to Have Died in Plane Crash Near Monterrey
2012-12-10.abstract: A plane carrying Jenni Rivera and four other passengers crashed early Sunday morning in the mountains of northeastern Mexico. No survivors were immediately found.

2012-12-11.title: Euro-Skeptics Turn Up Heat on Cameron
2012-12-11.abstract: An influential former cabinet minister said Monday that Britain should threaten to quit the European Union if Prime Minister David Cameron cannot negotiate looser ties.

2012-12-11.title: U.S. Places Militant Syrian Rebel Group on List of Terrorist Organizations
2012-12-11.abstract: The move was aimed at building Western support for the rebellion by quelling fears that money would end up in the hands of an group connected to Al Qaeda.

2012-12-11.title: Chávez in Cuba for Cancer Surgery
2012-12-11.abstract: As Hugo Chávez arrived in Havana, Venezuelans grappled with the prospect that their president may become too ill to be sworn in again next month.

2012-12-11.title: El Salvador: Court Orders Investigation of 1981 Massacre
2012-12-11.abstract: The Inter-American Court of Human Rights has ruled that a 20-year-old amnesty law does not cover war crimes committed during El Salvador’s long civil war.

2012-12-11.abstract: After a blaze that killed 112 workers in Bangladesh, Walmart said a single supplier had been operating there without authorization, but records suggest there were actually two.

2012-12-12.title: Immense Cache of Smuggled Ivory Is Seized in Malaysia
2012-12-12.abstract: The authorities found more than 1,000 smuggled tusks hidden in mahogany shipments, in one of the biggest seizures in history.

2012-12-12.title: Co-op Laws in Cuba Are Seen as Progress
2012-12-12.abstract: By calling for the creation of more than 200 cooperative businesses, the nation on Tuesday continued its process of opening its economy to free market ideas.

2012-12-12.title: North Koreans Launch Rocket in Defiant Act
2012-12-12.abstract: Washington and its allies have said they think North Korea’s rocket program has less to do with putting a satellite into orbit than with developing a vehicle for a nuclear warhead.

2012-12-12.title: Tour of Dioramas (Source: Jihad Museum)
2012-12-12.abstract: The museum in Herat, Afghanistan is a temple to the mujahedeen heroes who battled the Soviets in the 1970s and 1980s, and a portrayal of a likely future.

2012-12-12.abstract: The Jihad Museum honors Afghans who fought the Soviets in the 1970s and 1980s, a bloody time in the nation’s history that some believe is likely to repeat itself.

2012-12-13.title: Of Many Woes, One Man’s Illness Threatens Venezuela’s Revolution
2012-12-13.abstract: Many doubt that any successor will be as adept at fostering support, raising the question of what will become of President Hugo Chávez’s movement without him.

2012-12-13.title: Millions in Ransoms Fuel Militants’ Clout in West Africa
2012-12-13.abstract: Kidnapping is such a lucrative industry for extremists that it has reinforced their control over northern Mali and complicated plans for a campaign to take back Islamist-held territory.

2012-12-13.title: Members of Assad’s Sect Blamed in Syria Killings
2012-12-13.abstract: A pro-government Alawite militia is believed responsible for killing scores of other Alawites. Survivors said that victims were killed by a group that had been professing to defend them.

2012-12-13.title: Fighting Corruption, One Handyman at a Time
2012-12-13.abstract: Russians are crediting Roszkh, a new Web site created by a political activist, with helping them to get long-overdue building repairs taken care of.

2012-12-13.title: Kim Jong-un Earns Cachet With Rocket’s Success
2012-12-13.abstract: North Korea’s rocket launch reinforced the idea among its people that despite isolation and sanctions, the Kim dynasty is on the right track.

2012-12-14.title: If College Admissions Were Like Tetris, Everything Would Fall in Line
2012-12-14.abstract: Sush Krishnamoorthy, a student in New Delhi who is applying to universities in the United States, is bracing herself for rejection.

2012-12-14.title: Despite Risks, China Stays at North Korea’s Side to Keep the U.S. at Bay
2012-12-14.abstract: Analysts say China’s overriding fear is that a collapse of North Korea would bring reunification with the South and a flood of refugees over the Chinese border.

2012-12-14.title: Iran: U.S. Expands Anti-Nuclear Sanctions
2012-12-14.abstract: The seven blacklisted companies cannot do business with any American businesses or citizens and any assets they may have in the United States will be frozen.

2012-12-14.title: U.S. to Send 2 Missile Units to Turkey to Deter Syrians
2012-12-14.abstract: Turkey, which has been supporting the Syrian opposition to President Bashar al-Assad, has been worried that it was vulnerable to Syrian missiles.

2012-12-14.title: Putin Backs Penalizing U.S. Judges in Adoptions
2012-12-14.abstract: Sporadic cases of abuse, even deaths, of adopted Russian children in the United States have inflamed the Russian public.

2012-12-15.title: Obama Walks a Fine Line With Egyptian President
2012-12-15.abstract: As President Mohamed Morsi and his Muslim Brotherhood allies stood accused of authoritarianism, President Obama, via phone, urged him to offer his opposition compromises.

2012-12-15.title: Egypt Prepares to Vote on Draft Constitution
2012-12-15.abstract: Praying, protesting and waiting ahead of the country’s vote.

2012-12-15.title: A Political Pendulum in a Disgruntled Japan
2012-12-15.abstract: Voters in Japan, disappointed in the Democratic Party’s failure to reverse the nation’s malaise, appear poised to hand power back to the old-guard Liberal Democrats.

2012-12-15.abstract: Citing China’s own Constitution, nearly 100 scholars at universities and institutes around the world, from Melbourne to Warsaw to Vancouver, are asking Xi Jinping to reverse policies and practices allegedly destroying the Tibetan language and culture.

2012-12-15.abstract: Just as the euro replaced the German mark, the European Central Bank has supplanted the Bundesbank as the continent’s pre-eminent economic institution.

2012-12-16.title: China Calls for ‘No Delay’ on Gun Controls in U.S.
2012-12-16.abstract: China, which enforces a near-total ban on the private possession of firearms, has called for stricter gun controls in the United States. On the same day that a gunman killed 20 first-graders and six women at a school in Connecticut, a man in China stabbed 22 children and an 85-year-old woman. There were no fatalities in that attack, although some of the children reportedly had their ears and fingers cut off.

2012-12-16.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-12-16.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2012-12-16.title: First Round of Voting Spurs Dispute in Egypt
2012-12-16.abstract: The Muslim Brotherhood said that around 57 percent of those who cast ballots voted for the proposed constitution, but opposition leaders said voting was marred by irregularities.

2012-12-16.title: Japan Election Returns Power to Old Guard
2012-12-16.abstract: The Liberal Democratic Party was returned to power in parliamentary elections Sunday in what analysts say is more a vote against the incumbents than a mandate.

2012-12-16.title: A Syrian Airstrike Kills Palestinian Refugees and Costs Assad Support
2012-12-16.abstract: Government forces killed at least eight people in the Yarmouk district of Damascus and reportedly drove dozens of formerly pro-government Palestinian fighters to defect to the rebels.

2012-12-17.title: Good Name Is Restored in Terrain Known for Tea
2012-12-17.abstract: The European Union agreed this year to phase out the use of “Darjeeling” on blended teas, making tea growers in the real Darjeeling happy.

2012-12-17.title: Do Lower Taxes Create Jobs? Look at Clinton and Bush.

2012-12-17.title: Leadership Meeting in South Africa Confronts Bitter Divisions in the A.N.C.
2012-12-17.abstract: The African National Congress is deeply divided between those who want President Jacob Zuma elected for another term, and those seeking a fresh start.

2012-12-17.title: Fleeing Taxes and ‘Insult,’ Gérard Depardieu Says Au Revoir, La France
2012-12-17.abstract: In a public drama hardly imaginable anywhere other than France, the prime minister calls one of the most country’s greatest celebrities “pathetic” and the actor fires back, “I am surrendering my passport.”

2012-12-17.title: Troops Mass in Fought-Over City, Raising Fear of New Violence in Congo
2012-12-17.abstract: Witnesses reported that truckloads of Rwandan soldiers recently crossed the border and were camped within 20 miles of Goma, which was captured last month and briefly occupied by rebels.

2012-12-18.title: South Korea Vote Will Change Policy Toward North
2012-12-18.abstract: Park Geun-hye and Moon Jae-in, the top candidates in the South Korean presidential election, say they would take a softer approach toward North Korea than the departing president.

2012-12-18.title: West Bank Land, Empty but Full of Meaning
2012-12-18.abstract: A battle over E1, a largely empty patch of the West Bank, speaks to the seemingly insurmountable differences between the Israelis and the Palestinians.

2012-12-18.title: Fears That Lack of Money Will Weaken Malaria Battle
2012-12-18.abstract: The world has made great progress against malaria, but the fight is stalling for lack of funds, the World Health Organization’s annual report on malaria concluded on Monday.

2012-12-18.title: France: Multinational Military Force Expected for Mali
2012-12-18.abstract: France expects that the United Nations Security Council will authorize the deployment of a multinational African military force to regain control of the country’s north.

2012-12-18.title: Iraq: 30 Die in Bombings and Gunfire in Several Cities
2012-12-18.abstract: A wave of attacks across Iraq on Monday killed at least 30 people and wounded dozens more.

2012-12-19.title: The Fight for Aleppo
2012-12-19.abstract: A sustained and pitched battle between rebel fighters and government forces has left Aleppo in ruins.

2012-12-19.title: Rubble and Despair of War Redefine Syria Jewel
2012-12-19.abstract: Aleppo, Syria’s largest city and the bloodied stage for a battle now running into its sixth month, is administered by no one and slipping into disaster.

2012-12-19.abstract: Winter is descending on Aleppo, where an urban battle is being waged between rebels and the military of President Bashar al-Assad.

2012-12-19.title: Monks Lose Relevance as Thailand Grows Richer
2012-12-19.abstract: The country’s rapid economic rise has altered the role of the local Buddhist monk, once a moral authority, now someone whose job is often limited to presiding over ceremonies.

2012-12-19.title: For Modernizing Thais, Monastic Life Loses Allure
2012-12-19.abstract: Buddhist temples are growing lonely in rural Thailand as consumer culture rises.

2012-12-20.title: Neighborhood Is Torn Over a Killer’s Legacy
2012-12-20.abstract: For some in the hard-up immigrant neighborhood of Les Izards, Mohammed Merah is a martyr who expressed the anger and humiliation that many Muslims there feel.

2012-12-20.title: For One Russian Lawmaker, Bequeathing 31 ‘Aye’ Votes
2012-12-20.abstract: Vyacheslav K. Osipov, a governing party lawmaker, managed to cast the 31 votes in the lower house of Parliament despite being absent. And dead.

2012-12-20.title: Cayman Islands: Premier Is Named to Replace Ousted Predecessor
2012-12-20.abstract: The governor of the Cayman Islands on Wednesday appointed Juliana O’Connor-Connolly as the new premier of the British territory.

2012-12-20.title: Mexico: A Guzmán Is Deported
2012-12-20.abstract: A woman believed to be the daughter of the most-wanted drug trafficker in the world, Joaquín Guzmán Loera, has been deported to Mexico after two months in custody in California.

2012-12-20.title: Spain: Separatist Parties in Accord
2012-12-20.abstract: The two main separatist parties in Catalonia signed an agreement on Wednesday to allow the citizens of what is Spain’s most powerful economic region to vote in 2014 on whether to secede.

2012-12-21.title: Applicants Continue to Flock to Early Admission Programs
2012-12-21.abstract: One theme that emerges is that many binding early decision and nonbinding early action programs continue to record double-digit increases in applicants when compared with the previous year.

2012-12-21.title: France: Budget Proposal Approved
2012-12-21.abstract: The French Parliament approved the Socialist government’s 2013 budget proposal, a rigorous and contested plan to drop the deficit and increase taxes on individuals and businesses.

2012-12-21.title: Canada: Court Seeks a Balance on Veils
2012-12-21.abstract: The Supreme Court of Canada ruled Thursday that witnesses could cover their faces for religious reasons while testifying in court under some circumstances.

2012-12-21.title: Palestinian Premier Calls for Boycott of Israeli Goods
2012-12-21.abstract: Israel’s refusal this month to transfer tax revenue to the West Bank has undermined the Palestinian Authority’s already dire finances, the prime minister, Salam Fayyad, said.

2012-12-21.title: Georgia: A Stalinist Restoration
2012-12-21.abstract: The decision to restore a monument of Joseph Stalin was a rebuke to President Mikheil Saakashvili, who has tried to clear Georgia of all vestiges of Soviet symbolism.

2012-12-22.title: An Accidental Comedian of the People
2012-12-22.abstract: Ilka Bessin’s louder, ruder alter ego, Cindy from Marzahn, has helped this former chef become Germany’s female comedian of the year four times.

2012-12-22.title: In Syria, Cluster Munitions Takes Its Toll
2012-12-22.abstract: C.J. Chivers reports from Maraa, Syria, on the government’s use of cluster munitions against civilians in what appears to be collective punishment.

2012-12-22.title: China: Report Links Former Police Chief to Murder
2012-12-22.abstract: A Chinese news organization reported that Wang Lijun, a former police chief in Chongqing, played a direct role in organizing the murder of Neil Heywood, a British businessman.

2012-12-22.title: Cuba: National Assembly President to Leave His Post
2012-12-22.abstract: Ricardo Alarcón will leave his post as president of Cuba’s National Assembly in February after two decades, furthering a generational transition prioritized by President Raúl Castro.

2012-12-22.title: Tunisian Dictator’s Possessions to Be Sold at Public Auction
2012-12-22.abstract: Cars, jewelry, carpets and other assets that once belonged to the deposed president of Tunisia, Zine el-Abidine Ben Ali, will be auctioned at a monthlong sale and exhibition.

2012-12-23.title: At a Sacred Site, a Fight Over Women and Prayer
2012-12-23.abstract: After years of legislative and legal fights, the movement for equal access for people to pray as they wish at the Western Wall has become a rallying cause for liberal Jews.

2012-12-23.title: Women at the Western Wall
2012-12-23.abstract: Jodi Rudoren, The Times’s Jerusalem bureau chief, reports on Jewish women fighting Orthodox restrictions on how and where they can pray at the Western Wall in Jerusalem.

2012-12-23.title: Police Crackdown in Delhi Sunday After Protests Over Rape
2012-12-23.abstract: Attempt to contain growing anger over attacks on women.

2012-12-23.title: Adding More Bricks to the Great Firewall of China
2012-12-23.abstract: Chinese government censors appear to have reinforced their Internet firewall in recent days, blocking some of the leading services that allow people on the mainland to access forbidden sites like Twitter, Facebook and YouTube.

2012-12-23.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-12-23.abstract: Best of long-form journalism this week.

2012-12-24.title: China Takes Chilling Look at Security in Its Schools
2012-12-24.abstract: Video of a Dec. 14 attack that wounded 23 students in Henan Province has refocused attention on protective measures and efforts to squelch coverage.

2012-12-24.title: Official Killed in Bombing Is Mourned in Pakistan
2012-12-24.abstract: Bashir Ahman Bilour, a minister in Pakistan’s Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa Province who was killed in a suicide bombing, was buried in Peshawar.

2012-12-24.title: U.N. Says South Sudan Threatens Its Peacekeeping Mission
2012-12-24.abstract: The shooting down of a United Nations helicopter last week was just the latest in a series of attacks, a spokesman said.

2012-12-24.title: Citing Broken System, Critics Fight Russia’s Adoption Ban
2012-12-24.abstract: Critics say a ban on adoptions by Americans would end up further fraying a disastrously overwhelmed foster care and orphanage system in Russia.

2012-12-24.title: Where Did the Money Go?
2012-12-24.abstract: Public donors pledged $9.5 billion in relief and recovery aid to Haiti for 2010 to 2012. About $5.9 billion had been disbursed by the end of September, though disbursed does not always mean spent.

2012-12-25.title: Make Room for God, Pope Says at Christmas Eve Mass
2012-12-25.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI expressed concern for the place of faith in people’s busy lives, prayed for peace in the Middle East and called for an end to violence in Syria.

2012-12-25.title: Officials Push to Postpone Swearing-In of Chávez
2012-12-25.abstract: Aides to President Hugo Chávez, who is recuperating from cancer surgery in Cuba, say they will push back the inauguration scheduled for Jan. 10 if he is too ill to return to Venezuela.

2012-12-25.title: Troops in Afghanistan Find Cheer With a Yule Logon
2012-12-25.abstract: Online video chats and packages sent to and from home help Americans at a base in Kabul overcome their isolation at Christmas.

2012-12-25.title: No Easy Route if Assad Opts to Go, or to Stay, in Syria
2012-12-25.abstract: President Bashar al-Assad’s meeting with a United Nations envoy on Monday raised questions about how the Syrian leader will choose to respond to mounting pressure to step down.

2012-12-25.title: Frenzy Over Teaching Test Is Symptom of Italy’s Myriad Woes
2012-12-25.abstract: A flood of applicants for examinations to fill teaching positions said as much about Italy’s dim job prospects as it did about the rigidities of its public education system.

2012-12-26.title: Updating Mann’s Status From Stuffy to Chatty
2012-12-26.abstract: At the Buddenbrookhaus, a museum devoted to Thomas Mann, the challenge is how to keep a monumental, long-winded writer relevant in an age of shorter attention spans.

2012-12-26.title: First Fighting Islamists, Now the Free Market
2012-12-26.abstract: Hamdeen Sabahi has emerged as a leading voice in Egypt’s opposition, but economists warn his populist agenda could wreck Egypt’s economy.

2012-12-26.title: City in North Is Captured, Syrian Rebels Announce
2012-12-26.abstract: The fighters said they had taken control of Harem, a strategic town on the Turkish border, as the international envoy to Syria, Lakhdar Brahimi, remained in Damascus.

2012-12-26.title: Chinese Officials Find Misbehavior Now Carries Cost
2012-12-26.abstract: A flurry of revelations suggests that China’s new leadership may be serious about trying to tame the cronyism and bribery that afflicts state-run companies and local governments.

2012-12-26.title: Richard Baum, 72, Dies; Connected China Watchers on the Web
2012-12-26.abstract: Mr. Baum presided over Chinapol, an online discussion group about China that has become an essential forum for many experts, diplomats and journalists.

2012-12-27.title: South Africa: Mandela Is Released From the Hospital
2012-12-27.abstract: Nelson Mandela, South Africa’s former president, was released from the hospital late Wednesday, ending a 19-day hospital stay.

2012-12-27.title: Poaching of Jungle Elephants Strikes Gabon
2012-12-27.abstract: Once thought of as a model in protecting elephants from poachers, Gabon faces the same obstacles as other countries.

2012-12-27.title: In Gabon, Lure of Ivory Is Hard for Many to Resist
2012-12-27.abstract: Gabon’s government has made efforts to combat poaching, but as the price of ivory soars, its elephants are being slaughtered.

2012-12-27.title: When Prayer Really Counts in Air Travel
2012-12-27.abstract: A new directive prohibits all aircraft from flying across Iran during the call-to-prayer ritual, which is observed five times a day.

2012-12-27.title: Morsi Admits ‘Mistakes’ in Drafting Egypt’s Constitution
2012-12-27.abstract: President Mohamed Morsi appealed for unity after the bitter debate over the Constitution, and he pledged to respect the one-third of voters who cast ballots against it.

2012-12-28.title: A Plea From the Admissions Office to Go for the ‘Dangerous’ Essay
2012-12-28.abstract: An admissions officer advices students to tackle “dangerous questions” in their essays, “questions that require you to stake out a position, to have an opinion and to express something of substance.”

2012-12-28.title: Russia: Ex-Official Called in Graft Case
2012-12-28.abstract: Russian investigators have summoned a former defense minister for questioning in a corruption case involving the sale of land formerly owned by the military to a private company.

2012-12-28.title: The Battle for Aleppo
2012-12-28.abstract: As winter descends, the fighting’s direction has shifted in favor of the rebels, but it is unclear whether they can maintain an organized campaign.

2012-12-28.title: Battle for Syrian City Lays Both Sides’ Weaknesses Bare
2012-12-28.abstract: Growing rebel successes in the six-month fight for Aleppo have not come without setbacks, costs and questions about Syria’s future.

2012-12-28.title: Iran’s Slowing of Enrichment Efforts May Show It Wants a Deal, Analysts Say
2012-12-28.abstract: Some observers see signals in the Iranians’ diversion of medium-enriched uranium to a small research reactor.

2012-12-29.title: Iran: Navy Begins War Games Near Vital Waterway
2012-12-29.abstract: Iran, which periodically conducts naval maneuvers, began the war games Friday in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway considered vital to oil tanker traffic.

2012-12-29.title: Italy: Departing Leader Backs a New Centrist Coalition
2012-12-29.abstract: Mario Monti, the departing caretaker prime minister, said he would back a centrist grouping in national elections that are expected to place in February.

2012-12-29.title: Papers Show Rare Friction for Thatcher and Reagan
2012-12-29.abstract: Margaret Thatcher and Ronald Reagan agreed on many issues, but newly published documents show how the two strong allies butted heads over Britain’s 1982 Falklands war with Argentina.

2012-12-29.title: Eight Are Charged With Chilean Singer’s 1973 Murder After Military Coup
2012-12-29.abstract: Eight retired Chilean army officers have been charged with the 1973 murder of the singer, songwriter and theater director Víctor Jara in a Santiago stadium that now bears his name.

2012-12-29.title: Russian Adoption Ban Brings Uncertainty and Outrage
2012-12-29.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin signed the bill into law Friday, apparently blocking hundreds of orphans’ departure for the United States.

2012-12-30.title: Pakistan Lifts YouTube Ban, for 3 Minutes
2012-12-30.abstract: A ban imposed after an anti-Islam video caused riots was reinstated when it was discovered that blasphemous material was still available on the site.

2012-12-30.title: Drone Spy Wars
2012-12-30.abstract: Al Qaeda and Taliban-supported death squads accuse tribesmen of collaborating with the United States, and often force them to confess on video before executing them.

2012-12-30.title: Drone War Spurs Militants to Deadly Reprisals
2012-12-30.abstract: Militants in Pakistan’s tribal belt tape confessions and executions of men accused of aiding drone attacks, deepening an atmosphere of paranoia and mistrust.

2012-12-30.title: Coming Soon to Belgian Village, a French Film Idol Fleeing Taxes
2012-12-30.abstract: Néchin, a rural settlement in Belgium, has become a tax haven for scores of wealthy French citizens, including, most recently, the actor Gérard Depardieu.

2012-12-30.title: Trending in Asia in 2012: Our Top 10
2012-12-30.abstract: From the best — and worst — countries to be a woman, to human and animal rights, to China, China, China, our most read and commented posts on Asia.

2012-12-31.title: A Factory on Bicycle Wheels, Carrying Hope of a Better Life
2012-12-31.abstract: Amor Muñoz’s mobile sewing factory is about community and “the experience of art” as much as economic development.

2012-12-31.title: For Liberals in Egypt, a Champion Who Quips
2012-12-31.abstract: Bassem Youssef has used parody to argue that Egypt’s ultraconservative sheiks are distorting Islam, and for the moment, his satire appears to have trumped their sanctimony.

2012-12-31.title: Envoy to Syria Warns of Slide to Hellish Fiefs With Huge Toll
2012-12-31.abstract: The international envoy, Lakhdar Brahimi, said that if fighting continued, it could lead to 100,000 deaths in the next year and a territorial free-for-all.

2012-12-31.title: In Kabul, Deadly Cold Returns
2012-12-31.abstract: Afghan refugees living in Kabul camps battle deadly cold as winter sets in.

2012-12-31.title: Indian Women March: ‘That Girl Could Have Been Any One of Us’
2012-12-31.abstract: Protesters say they and others like them will never fully take part in the promise of a more prosperous nation unless something fundamental changes.
